title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 40 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: Poland's official public holiday marking the start of the new year with celebrations across the country.

festivals.name: Epiphany
festivals.description: Religious holiday commemorating the visit of the Magi to Jesus, celebrated with church services and traditional customs.

festivals.name: Winter Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Kraków's prestigious jazz festival featuring world-class musicians and performances in historic venues.

festivals.name: Paczki Day
festivals.description: Fat Tuesday celebration featuring paczki pastries, the most indulgent day before Lent with parades and festivities.

festivals.name: Carnival Season
festivals.description: Traditional pre-Lenten celebrations with masked balls, parades, and street festivities across Silesian cities.

festivals.name: Independence Day
festivals.description: Polish public holiday commemorating Poland's independence regained in 1918, celebrated nationwide with ceremonies and flag displays.

festivals.name: International Film Festival New Horizons
festivals.description: Prestigious arthouse cinema festival in Szczecin showcasing avant-garde and experimental films from around the world.

festivals.name: Good Friday
festivals.description: Religious holiday observed with church services and processions commemorating Christ's crucifixion.

festivals.name: Easter
festivals.description: Major Christian holiday celebrated with church services, family gatherings, and traditional Easter baskets blessed in churches.

festivals.name: Easter Monday
festivals.description: Traditional water-throwing day called Smigus Dyngus with playful celebrations and public holidays across Poland.

festivals.name: Labour Day
festivals.description: International public holiday in Poland marked by parades, gatherings, and celebrations of workers' rights.

festivals.name: Constitution Day
festivals.description: National holiday commemorating Poland's 1791 Constitution, one of Europe's first, with official ceremonies and remembrance.

festivals.name: Corpus Christi Processions
festivals.description: Major religious processions in Catholic communities, particularly elaborate in Kraków, celebrating the Eucharist.

festivals.name: Wianki Festival
festivals.description: Warsaw's enchanting Midsummer festival with floating flower wreaths on the Vistula River and traditional celebrations.

festivals.name: St. John's Night Celebrations
festivals.description: Midsummer festival with bonfires, traditional dances, and pagan-rooted customs celebrated across Poland.

festivals.name: Poznań International Theatre Festival
festivals.description: Europe's leading theatre festival showcasing cutting-edge performances from Poland and around the world.

festivals.name: Assumption of Mary
festivals.description: Major religious holiday celebrated nationwide with church services and a public holiday, especially revered in Polish Catholic tradition.

festivals.name: Piernik Festival
festivals.description: Toruń's famous gingerbread festival celebrating the city's centuries-old tradition of piernik production with tastings and crafts.

festivals.name: Warsaw Summer Jazz Days
festivals.description: Outdoor jazz festival in Warsaw's parks and streets featuring international and Polish artists in a relaxed summer atmosphere.

festivals.name: Gdańsk Shakespeare Festival
festivals.description: Annual theatre festival in Gdańsk presenting Shakespeare's works and contemporary interpretations in historic venues.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day
festivals.description: Polish public holiday when families visit cemeteries to remember deceased loved ones, marked by flower offerings and candlelit vigils.

festivals.name: Independence Day Celebrations
festivals.description: National public holiday commemorating Poland's independence from imperial rule in 1918, celebrated with parades and ceremonies nationwide.

festivals.name: Warsaw Autumn International Festival of Contemporary Music
festivals.description: One of Europe's premier contemporary music festivals featuring avant-garde compositions and innovative performances.

festivals.name: Kraków Film Festival
festivals.description: International film festival celebrating Polish and world cinema with screenings in historic Kraków venues.

festivals.name: Off Plus Camera International Film Festival
festivals.description: Kraków's avant-garde film festival showcasing experimental and cutting-edge cinema from around the world.

festivals.name: Manggha Museum of Japanese Art and Technology Opening Season
festivals.description: Year-round cultural events and exhibitions at Kraków's unique Japanese art museum showcasing traditional and contemporary works.

festivals.name: Christmas Eve
festivals.description: Polish public holiday marked by Wigilia family gatherings with traditional meatless feasts and religious observances.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: Christian public holiday celebrating the birth of Christ with church services and family celebrations throughout Poland.

festivals.name: Second Day of Christmas
festivals.description: Polish public holiday continuing Christmas celebrations with church services and family gatherings.

festivals.name: Wroclove Street Art Festival
festivals.description: Wrocław's celebrated street art festival transforming the city into an open-air gallery with murals and urban art.

festivals.name: New Music Festival
festivals.description: Wrocław's avant-garde music festival featuring contemporary composers and experimental musical performances.

festivals.name: Jewish Summer Festival
festivals.description: Kraków's vibrant festival celebrating Jewish culture, history, and contemporary arts with concerts, films, and exhibitions.

festivals.name: Camerimage International Film Festival
festivals.description: World's leading cinematography festival in Toruń attracting filmmakers and cinematographers from around the globe.

festivals.name: Sopot International Song Festival
festivals.description: Poland's most prestigious music festival featuring international and Polish pop, rock, and artistic performers since 1961.

festivals.name: Amber Festival
festivals.description: Gdańsk's celebration of amber with exhibitions, crafts, and demonstrations of this precious Baltic fossil.

festivals.name: Motława River Nights
festivals.description: Gdańsk's summer festival featuring river illuminations, music, and cultural performances along the historic waterfront.

festivals.name: Biennale of Contemporary Art
festivals.description: Wrocław's major contemporary art exhibition showcasing innovative works from Polish and international artists.

festivals.name: Lublin Easter Bazaar
festivals.description: Traditional Easter market in Lublin featuring crafts, folk art, and regional specialties in festive atmosphere.

festivals.name: Białystok Summer Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Northeast Poland's premier jazz festival in Białystok featuring international and Polish jazz artists.

festivals.name: Fringe Festival
festivals.description: Warsaw's experimental theatre and arts festival showcasing innovative and unconventional performances.
